Near Record Warmth

Last Updated Fri March 06 at 11:50AM by Wayne Presnell

It is time to get that fishing gear ready to go. The warm temperatures we've been talking about so much recently continue through the middle of next week. Today's record high for Boone is 72F so we could set a new record today. It is earlier than usual, but we are now in an afternoon shower regime. Isolated afternoon showers are possible today with the coverage increasing for Saturday. We may hear a rumble of thunder Saturday. Sunday brings more shower chances as a cold front fades out over our region. We see more sun Monday but afternoon shower chances continue.

Forecast Discussion

The Bermuda High controls our weather through Saturday afternoon with warm temperatures and isolated afternoon showers. The record high temperature for Boone today is 72F, set in 1945. We could exceed that today. More record warmth is possible next week.

A cold front approaches our area Sunday morning but begins to fade. It does increase the chances for showers and thunder Saturday into Sunday morning but it doesn't cool temperatures any.

Temperatures are a little cooler Sunday afternoon due to an increase in clouds but bump back up Monday and Tuesday due to more sunshine. Shower coverage returns to isolated Monday and Tuesday as the Bermuda High establishes control again.

A cold front is expected to approach the area Wednesday and pass through Thursday morning. This front brings an increase in rain and thunder chances and much cooler air behind it. Thursday will feel cold compared to this recent stretch of warmth but temperatures will be close to or slightly above average.

Signals indicate a return to consistently colder weather for the second half of the month as winter takes another shot at us.
